title: Implement unified server hot-path cache layer (Rec#1 + Rec#2 + Rec#13)
description: Consolidate three overlapping I/O optimizations into a single ContextCache class: (1) convert readAgentsMeta from sync to async with TTL cache + chokidar invalidation, (2) replace audit.jsonl O(n) full-read with a sliding-window byte-offset read, (3) wire loadGetRulesContext as cache-first (meta + AGENTS.md + human-lock). This eliminates the primary hot-path bottleneck: ~30 redundant I/O operations per 10-file edit cycle.

changes: Create ContextCache class with three cache slots: GetRulesContext (TTL), Rule LRU, Audit sliding-window
Wire chokidar file-watch events from http.ts to cache invalidation
Wrap return behind cache.get('meta') with TTL (e.g. 5s)
Invalidate on update_registry write and chokidar watch event
Wrap loadGetRulesContext as cache-first: return cached context if valid, else run 3 I/O and populate cache
Cache key includes projectRoot + revision hash
Invalidate slot when meta-reader cache invalidates
Rewrite readAuditLog to use byte-offset sliding-window read (last N bytes / last 5-minute window), mirroring the pattern in events.ts
Optional: add periodic rotation trigger when file exceeds size threshold
Cache last-read byte offset in ContextCache audit slot
Share/expose existing chokidar watcher instance to ContextCache for coordinated invalidation
Avoid creating a second watcher if one already exists

implementation: Read existing chokidar watcher pattern in http.ts and event replay pattern in events.ts to understand byte-offset approach
Create packages/server/src/cache.ts with ContextCache class: TTL map for meta/rules, sliding-window cursor for audit
Refactor meta-reader.ts: readFileSync → async readFile, wrap with cache.get/set, expose cache-bust on write
Refactor services/get-rules.ts loadGetRulesContext: add cache.get before I/O, populate on miss, share invalidation with meta-reader
Refactor services/audit-log.ts readAuditLog: read only from last stored byte offset or last 5-min window
Wire cache invalidation into http.ts chokidar watcher and update_registry write path
Run existing test suite to verify no regression in tool call responses

reference.pattern: Byte-offset sliding window (events.ts replay), chokidar watcher (http.ts)
reference.examples: Copy the byte-offset pattern from events.ts JsonlEventStore; adapt for audit.jsonl

rationale.chosen_approach: Unified ContextCache class as single invalidation owner, reusing existing chokidar watcher
alternatives_considered: Implement Rec#1, Rec#2, Rec#13 as three separate PRs (would require three watcher hookup passes)
Use Redis/LRU-cache npm package (unnecessary external dep for in-process cache)
decision_factors: conclusions.json explicitly marks Rec#13 as 'unification/refactor task if Rec#1 & Rec#2 already land'
Shared chokidar instance avoids double file-watching overhead
Single invalidation point reduces race condition surface
rationale.tradeoffs: Slightly larger initial change; compensated by eliminating three separate invalidation hookups

risks.description: Cache invalidation timing: update_registry write completes before chokidar fires, causing stale read on immediate next tool call
risks.mitigation: Invalidate cache eagerly at write site (update_registry service) in addition to watcher callback
risks.fallback: Add 50ms debounce or version counter to detect staleness

risks.description: Sliding-window audit read may miss entries if window is set too narrow
risks.mitigation: Default window = 5 minutes matching typical edit cycle; make configurable
risks.fallback: Fall back to full read if byte-offset seek fails or file was rotated

criteria: readAgentsMeta is async (no readFileSync call in meta-reader.ts)
10 consecutive fab_get_rules calls produce exactly 1 complete I/O (verified via cache hit counter or test spy)
readAuditLog execution time is O(1) relative to file size (byte-offset seek, not full read)
All existing MCP tool integration tests pass without modification
cache.ts exports ContextCache class with get/set/invalidate API
convergence.verification: pnpm test --filter server; check no readFileSync in meta-reader.ts via grep; measure audit-log read time with a 1000-entry fixture
convergence.definition_of_done: Server hot path no longer blocks on synchronous file reads and audit log reads scale O(1) with file size

unit: ContextCache: TTL expiry evicts entry after timeout
readAuditLog: returns only entries within sliding window
readAuditLog: byte offset advances correctly on consecutive calls
manual_checks: Start MCP server, trigger 10 consecutive fab_get_rules calls, confirm only 1 actual file read in server logs
Append 500 lines to audit.jsonl, confirm readAuditLog still returns near-instant
success_metrics: Cache hit rate > 90% on typical 10-file edit cycle
readAuditLog latency < 5ms regardless of audit.jsonl size

commit.message_template: refactor(server): unified ContextCache — async meta-reader, audit sliding-window, cache-first get-rules
